author: śrīla viśvanātha cakrabartī ṭhākura
verses:
vande śrī⋅vṛṣabhānu⋅sutā⋅pada
kamalanayana locanasampada

nakhara mukurajita koṭi sudhākara
mādhava⋅hṛdaya⋅cakora manohara

kamala⋅nuta saubhaga⋅rekhāñcita
lalitādi⋅kara⋅yāvaka⋅rañjita

saṁsevya giridhara mati maṇḍita
rāsa⋅vilāsa⋅naṭana⋅rasa paṇḍita
en_translation:
I worship the feet of the daughter of Śrī Vṛṣabhānu, whose lotus eyes are a treasure of beauty.

Her toenails are like mirrors that conquer millions of moons, captivating to the heart of Lord Mādhava.

Adorned with auspicious lines of beauty, She is painted with red lac dye by Lalitā and Her other friends.

Adorned with devotion that is served by the holder of the Hill, She is an expert in the mellows of the Rāsa dance.
